2013-05-01.title: Kerry Calls Arab League Plan to Revive Talks With Israel a ‘Big Step’
2013-05-01.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry called a new initiative “a very big step forward,” but initial reactions suggested that the plan might have difficulty penetrating a years-long impasse.

2013-05-01.title: Leader of Hezbollah Warns It Is Ready to Come to Syria’s Aid
2013-05-01.abstract: The leader of Hezbollah edged closer to acknowledging that its fighters were battling rebels in Syria, an intervention that threatens to drag Lebanon deeper into that conflict.

2013-05-01.title: The Most Hated Bangladeshi, Toppled From a Shady Empire
2013-05-01.abstract: Sohel Rana is under arrest after the collapse of his factory building last week left nearly 400 people dead, but until now he has been a powerful figure, trailed by his own biker gang.

2013-05-01.title: Retailers Split on Contrition After Collapse of Factories
2013-05-01.abstract: Several apparel companies sought to minimize any connections to the building in Bangladesh, while some others quickly acknowledged theirs.

2013-05-01.title: Guatemala: Former Dictator’s Genocide Trial Resumes
2013-05-01.abstract: The genocide trial of a former dictator of Guatemala, Gen. Efraín Ríos Montt, resumed Tuesday, 12 days after a judge ruled to annul the court proceedings.

2013-05-02.title: May Day Protests Around the World
2013-05-02.abstract: Workers around the globe observed the international holiday with demonstrations and rallies.

2013-05-02.title: Amid Fealty to Socialism, a Nod to Capitalism
2013-05-02.abstract: On May Day in Havana, among the throngs of state employees marching was a novel and increasingly favored breed: private entrepreneurs and employees.

2013-05-02.title: U.S. Agency Is Expelled From Bolivia
2013-05-02.abstract: President Evo Morales suggested that the United States Agency for International Development had conspired against his leftist government.

2013-05-02.title: Immigration Is on Agenda for Obama in Mexico
2013-05-02.abstract: President Obama planned to discuss economic and trade matters, the drug war and immigration, the most delicate topic, when he meets with President Enrique Peña Nieto.

2013-05-02.title: Political Chaos Grips Venezuela After Legislative Brawl and Rival Marches
2013-05-02.abstract: Tensions have remained high since Nicolás Maduro, the handpicked heir of Hugo Chávez, won the presidential election by fewer than two percentage points.

2013-05-03.title: U.S. Lifts Ban on Visas for Burmese Officials
2013-05-03.abstract: Lifting the ban is the latest effort by the Obama administration to reward Myanmar after its president, Thein Sein, began to open the country’s political system and economy in 2011.

2013-05-03.title: Venezuela: Legal Challenge to Vote
2013-05-03.abstract: Lawyers for the opposition candidate in the recent presidential election, Henrique Capriles Radonski, on Thursday submitted papers to the Supreme Court challenging the result.

2013-05-03.title: Bangladesh Arrests Engineer Who Warned of Dangers
2013-05-03.abstract: The arrest of the engineer, Abdur Razzaque Khan, was a surprise twist since he was regarded as something of a hero for trying to avert the April 24 disaster.

2013-05-03.title: A Growing Egypt
2013-05-03.abstract: The country’s birthrate has surged to a 20-year high, overwhelming the most populous Arab state’s resources.

2013-05-03.title: Egypt’s Birthrate Rises as Population Control Policies Vanish
2013-05-03.abstract: President Mohamed Morsi has remained silent about the future of the family planning programs put in place by the government of former President Hosni Mubarak.

2013-05-04.title: Revival of Europe’s Economy Falls to German Leader
2013-05-04.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany faces the task of getting Europe growing again while continuing to advocate her austerity policies to the German electorate.

2013-05-04.title: A Grandson Visits China in Footsteps of Nixon
2013-05-04.abstract: The trip by Christopher Nixon Cox, 100 years after President Richard M. Nixon’s birth, was organized to lionize both the president’s and the Communist Party’s accomplishments.

2013-05-04.title: Prison Conditions Eased for Ex-Marine Held in Iran
2013-05-04.abstract: Relatives see possible signs that Iran eventually could release a man who has been held there for nearly two years on suspicions of spying.

2013-05-04.title: Judge Says Cuban Who Spied on U.S. Can Stay in Cuba
2013-05-04.abstract: A federal judge in Miami said a convicted spy could stay in Cuba, where he went for his father’s funeral, if he renounced his American citizenship.

2013-05-04.title: A Sadrist Governor Is a Folk Hero to Iraqis
2013-05-04.abstract: For Iraqis frustrated with poor services, sectarian politics and violence, the governor, Ali Dwai, provides a rare example of democracy’s potential.

2013-05-05.title: Song Critical of Pakistani Generals Is Blocked Online, With No Official Explanation
2013-05-05.abstract: No official explanation has been given for the apparent ban on the song, which critics have praised for its sharp humor.

2013-05-05.title: Life in North Korean Complex: A Glimmer of Hope
2013-05-05.abstract: The suspension of operations at a joint North and South Korean factory complex has dimmed optimism that the two nations were trying to find common ground.

2013-05-05.title: Off-the-Cuff Obama Line Put U.S. in Bind on Syria
2013-05-05.abstract: President Obama surprised aides last year when he called the use of chemical weapons in Syria a “red line,” a move that has complicated the American response to evidence that such arms have been used.

2013-05-05.title: Divided Over Bangladesh
2013-05-05.abstract: A look at two companies with different responses to the tragedy in Bangladesh.

2013-05-05.title: Australia Strives to Balance China and the United States
2013-05-05.abstract: Reactions in the official Chinese media to Australia’s new defense White Paper on China, which is more conciliatory than a previous one, were low-key, if apparently approving. Reactions from ordinary Chinese, expressed online, were mixed but still often critical.

2013-05-06.title: Malaysia’s Governing Coalition Keeps Hold on Power
2013-05-06.abstract: The coalition won a majority of seats in Parliament in national elections, but opposition parties claimed that the result was tainted by voting irregularities.

2013-05-06.title: Merkel’s Flagging Coalition Partner Tries to Bolster Its Image
2013-05-06.abstract: The struggling junior partner in Germany’s coalition government with Chancellor Angela Merkel’s Christian Democrats held a party congress that offered an election plan intended to attract voters.

2013-05-06.title: Extremists Pursue Mainstream in Pakistan Election
2013-05-06.abstract: With a name change, a banned group is fielding candidates across Pakistan, and though few are expected to win Parliament seats, experts note the flexing of political muscle.

2013-05-06.title: Israeli Attacks on Syria Fuel Debate Over U.S.-Led Effort
2013-05-06.abstract: The United States, Britain and France discussed the possibility of American-led strikes privately in the days before the attacks linked to Israel, officials said.

2013-05-06.title: New Law in Libya Bans Some From Office
2013-05-06.abstract: Bowing to pressure from armed Islamists and other militiamen, the congress passed a law to exclude former officials of the Qaddafi era from public office.

2013-05-07.title: Video of Police Hunt for Drug Trafficker Raises Concern in Brazil
2013-05-07.abstract: The video, taken from a Rio de Janeiro police helicopter and depicting officers spraying the suspect’s car with gunfire in a densely populated area, raised concerns over the heavy use of force by the police.

2013-05-07.title: Israel Allows Gazan Children to Visit Imprisoned Parents
2013-05-07.abstract: For the first time since 2006, young Gazans under age 8 were allowed on Monday to visit their parents in Israeli prisons.

2013-05-07.title: From Jungle, Brazil Aims to Extend Its Reach
2013-05-07.abstract: A remote training center, where soldiers from around the world swim in piranha-infested waters and endure jungle hikes without rations, is helping Brazil raise its profile.

2013-05-07.title: Brazilian Military’s Crucible: Jungle Warfare Instruction
2013-05-07.abstract: In courses lasting about nine weeks, soldiers endure long hikes, swim in waters infested with piranha and survive for days without rations, hunting or foraging for their own food.

2013-05-07.title: Intelligence on North Korea, and Its New Leader, Remains Elusive
2013-05-07.abstract: At a time when the United States has learned to target drone strikes with increasing accuracy and direct cyberweapons at specific nuclear centrifuges, its understanding of North Korea has gotten worse.

2013-05-08.title: China Blasts Hacking Claim by Pentagon
2013-05-08.abstract: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs criticized a Pentagon report that accused China’s military of cyberattacks on the American government and military contractors.

2013-05-08.title: Iraq’s Worsening Sunni Protests Revolve Around Antiterrorism Tactics
2013-05-08.abstract: United States-imposed laws are central to the protest movement, as those who once helped the American military now sometimes put innocent people in prison in their work as informants for the Iraqi government.

2013-05-08.title: Mexico: Gas Tanker Explodes; 22 Dead
2013-05-08.abstract: A double tanker transporting gas struck a containment wall on a highway in a Mexico City suburb on Tuesday and burst into flames, killing 22 people and injuring 31.

2013-05-08.title: Hezbollah Takes Risks by Fighting Rebels in Syria
2013-05-08.abstract: Trained to battle Israel, Hezbollah’s Lebanese Shiite guerrillas are pushing into a very different fight, against fellow Arab Muslims trying to topple President Bashar al-Assad.

2013-05-08.title: Obama Backs South Korea President’s Policy on North
2013-05-08.abstract: President Obama endorsed President Park Geun-hye’s plans for defusing tensions with North Korea, but warned that the first move was up to Kim Jong-un.

2013-05-09.title: Court Upholds Berlusconi Tax Fraud Verdict
2013-05-09.abstract: An appeals court in Milan sentenced former Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi to four years in prison and imposed a five-year ban on his holding public office.

2013-05-09.title: Israel Moves to End Gender Segregation in Public Spaces
2013-05-09.abstract: The attorney general called on public agencies “to act fast, efficiently and decisively” on sweeping new rules after years of legal battles over the treatment of women.

2013-05-09.title: Preparing for Watershed Elections in Pakistan
2013-05-09.abstract: In a country where politicians have competed with military generals for power, patronage is a prominent tool of civilian influence.

2013-05-09.title: Allegations of Corruption Swirl Against Sarkozy Ally
2013-05-09.abstract: The scandal involving Claude Guéant,  a former interior minister, involves a bank transfer, two Flemish paintings and tens of thousands of dollars in cash payments.

2013-05-09.title: Boston Bomb Inquiry Looks Closely at Russia Trip
2013-05-09.abstract: Officials are investigating reports that Tamerlan Tsarnaev, a suspect in the Boston Marathon bombings, may have tried to contact Islamist extremists.

2013-05-10.title: Suspect in Boston Bombing Talked Jihad in Russia
2013-05-10.abstract: Acquaintances of Tamerlan Tsarnaev in Russia said he was already interested in waging jihad when he arrived there last year, but they discouraged him from joining a militant group.

2013-05-10.title: U.S. Imposes Sanctions on Those Aiding Iran
2013-05-10.abstract: The actions appeared to signal an accelerated effort by the United States to squeeze Iran economically over the lack of progress in negotiations on its disputed nuclear program.

2013-05-10.title: In Its Efforts to Integrate Roma, Slovakia Recalls U.S. Struggles
2013-05-10.abstract: Eastern Slovakia’s elementary schools are a microcosm of one of Europe’s biggest challenges: how to bring its most disadvantaged minority into the mainstream.

2013-05-10.title: Slovakia Struggles to Desegregate Its Schools
2013-05-10.abstract: An elementary school in eastern Slovakia is a microcosm of one of Europe’s biggest challenges: how to bring its most disadvantaged and fastest-growing minority into the mainstream.

2013-05-10.title: Ex-Dictator Denies Role in Guatemalan Massacres
2013-05-10.abstract: In a rambling statement on Thursday lasting almost an hour, Gen. Efraín Ríos Montt said he had never authorized or proposed attacks on any ethnic groups.

2013-05-11.title: Egypt: Prominent Activist Jailed
2013-05-11.abstract: Egyptian activist Ahmed Maher was arrested on accusations of inciting protests in front of the Interior Ministry in March and was transferred to a high-security prison.

2013-05-11.title: Former Leader of Guatemala Is Guilty of Genocide Against Mayan Group
2013-05-11.abstract: The judge who sentenced former dictator Gen. Efraín Ríos Montt to 80 years in prison said the court was “convinced of the intent to destroy the Ixil ethnic group.”

2013-05-11.title: With Many Despairing, Bulgaria Heads to Polls
2013-05-11.abstract: Six self-immolations since February, and the resignation of Prime Minister Boiko Borisov, have characterized the mood of the nation as it prepares to vote.

2013-05-11.title: Rodman Says He’ll Ask North Korea to Free American
2013-05-11.abstract: Dennis Rodman told TMZ that he planned to return to North Korea on Aug. 1 to try to free Kenneth Bae, a Korean-American sentenced to 15 years of hard labor.

2013-05-11.title: After Malaysia Election, Political Attacks Continue as Opposition Calls for Protests
2013-05-11.abstract: Analysts say that the political turmoil since Sunday’s national election brings concern of a potentially explosive showdown between the prime minister and the opposition leader.

2013-05-12.title: Out of Egypt’s Chaos, Musical Rebellion
2013-05-12.abstract: Egypt’s rising-star rappers predated the revolution that ousted President Hosni Mubarak, but the turmoil since has left a huge youth population searching for voices that address issues they care about.

2013-05-12.title: Egypt’s Music Revolution
2013-05-12.abstract: A youth-driven and socially conscious music genre called mahraganat reflects social shifts spurred by the revolution that toppled President Hosni Mubarak in 2011.

2013-05-12.title: Lao She’s Art Collection Auctioned Amid Enthusiasm
2013-05-12.abstract: A collection of paintings belonging to the writer Lao She, who killed himself in the Cultural Revolution, and his wife, the painter Hu Jieqing, were auctioned in Beijing amid considerable excitement.

2013-05-12.title: South Korea Seeks Journalist’s Arrest in Defamation Case
2013-05-12.abstract: Prosecutors said Choo Chin-woo had “spread false information” through articles and a podcast linking President Park Geun-hye’s brother to a relative’s killing.

2013-05-12.title: After a Journalist’s Prodding, China Investigates a Top Official
2013-05-12.abstract: Investigators said a senior economic policy maker, Liu Tienan, was “suspected of grave violations of discipline,” accusations first raised by a magazine editor.

2013-05-13.title: Afghans Say an American Tortured Civilians
2013-05-13.abstract: An arrest warrant has been issued for a man that Afghan officials say is an American and part of a Special Forces unit operating in Wardak Province.

2013-05-13.title: Officials Say Homophobia Motivated Murder in Russia
2013-05-13.abstract: Three men were arrested in connection with the death of a 23-year-old man in Volgograd, and the findings were a rare acknowledgment during a period of rising antigay sentiment from officials.

2013-05-13.title: Cyberattacks Against U.S. Corporations Are on the Rise
2013-05-13.abstract: Officials said the aim in a new wave of attacks was not espionage but sabotage, and that the source seemed to be in the Middle East.

2013-05-13.title: Pakistani Party Leader Looks Forward, as Claims of Vote-Rigging Swirl
2013-05-13.abstract: Former Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif was projected to be the big winner in Saturday’s election, which was marred by violence and reports of fraud at the polls.

2013-05-13.title: IHT Quick Read: May 13
2013-05-13.abstract: Turmoil after the Pakistan election; countries lobby in Washington’s immigration debate; Chinese automakers put down roots in Detroit; renewed appreciation for Buckminster Fuller; Nadal and Williams triumph in Madrid.

2013-05-14.title: Women Forced Into WWII Brothels Served Necessary Role, Osaka Mayor Says
2013-05-14.abstract: Toru Hashimoto’s view that so-called comfort women provided relief for Japanese soldiers “risking their lives” was swiftly condemned by lawmakers and human rights groups.

2013-05-14.title: A Colorful Bolivian Bastion, Floating Above It All
2013-05-14.abstract: Open displays of wealth are often embraced in El Alto, an illustration of the city’s unusual mix of leftist uprisings and capitalist strivings.

2013-05-14.title: France Confirms Second Case of Virus Linked to SARS
2013-05-14.abstract: The cases are being watched closely because the transmission occurred in a shared hospital room.

2013-05-14.title: Sharif vs. Army, Round 3
2013-05-14.abstract: Nawaz Sharif’s previous term as prime minister ended when he was deposed by the military, and his new tenure may depend on how he gets along with the generals.

2013-05-14.title: 2 Waiters Arrested in Killing of Malcolm X’s Grandson
2013-05-14.abstract: The men taken into custody worked at a bar in Mexico City where Malcolm Shabazz was beaten during what a prosecutor called a dispute over an excessive bill.

2013-05-15.title: An Atrocity in Syria, With No Victim Too Small
2013-05-15.abstract: A mass killing has revealed new depths of ruthlessness in Syria’s conflict, inflaming sectarian divisions and making any hope of reconciliation appear ever more distant.

2013-05-15.title: Afghan Commandos Step Up Their Combat Role
2013-05-15.abstract: A combination of allied Special Operations forces and Afghan troops are set to assume more responsibility in Afghanistan as NATO gradually hands over security operations.

2013-05-15.title: Iranian Officials Threaten Two Candidates for the Presidency
2013-05-15.abstract: The political establishment has not responded well to Ali Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani and Esfandiar Rahim Mashaei, who entered the race at the last minute.

2013-05-15.title: French Lawmakers Loosen Labor Rules in a Victory for the President
2013-05-15.abstract: The measures, an important win for President François Hollande, give employers more flexibility to lay off employees, but also contain concessions for workers.

2013-05-15.title: Brazilian Court Council Removes a Barrier to Same-Sex Marriage
2013-05-15.abstract: The council ruled on Tuesday that notary publics could not refuse to perform same-sex marriage ceremonies, a decision that effectively legalizes gay marriage.

2013-05-16.title: U.N. Calls for Political Transition in Syria
2013-05-16.abstract: The nonbinding resolution called for a transition to end the civil war in Syria, but members of the United Nations recognized that it was unlikely to stem the violence.

2013-05-16.title: Mexico: Official Whose Daughter Forced Restaurant Closing Is Fired
2013-05-16.abstract: The head of the federal consumer protection agency was fired Wednesday, weeks after his daughter got inspectors to briefly shut down a restaurant that refused her the table she wanted.

2013-05-16.title: Nigeria’s President Gives Military More Power in Struggle Against Militants
2013-05-16.abstract: Troops have begun deploying in the wake of President Goodluck Jonathan’s declaration of a state of emergency in parts of the north, but critics fear more civilian deaths.

2013-05-16.title: Mandela Fades Amid Battles Over Who Will Claim Legacy
2013-05-16.abstract: As Nelson Mandela, 94, grows more fragile, the struggle in South Africa over how he will be remembered, and what he has to pass on, has become increasingly acrimonious.

2013-05-16.title: For Troops Needing Psychological Help, Asking Can Be the Toughest Hurdle
2013-05-16.abstract: A Marine veteran haunted by his service writes that the time has come to change the perception of post-traumatic stress disorder to allow greater honesty and healing.

2013-05-17.title: Performing From Space, Canada’s Low-Orbit Star
2013-05-17.abstract: Chris Hadfield’s exploits in space may have cemented his reputation as the world’s best-known singing astronaut, not to mention Canada’s newest celebrity.

2013-05-17.title: As Japan Courts Growth, Europe Keeps Up Its Love Affair With Austerity
2013-05-17.abstract: Japan’s economy has posted a robust growth rate, but economists said Europe, particularly Germany under Chancellor Angela Merkel, was not willing to learn from Tokyo.

2013-05-17.title: Greece: Teachers Abandon Strike Plan
2013-05-17.abstract: Greek high school teachers on Thursday called off a strike after the government essentially banned it by enacting an emergency law that threatened strikers with arrest or dismissal.

2013-05-17.title: Syria Begins to Break Apart Under Pressure From War
2013-05-17.abstract: A constellation of armed groups battling to advance their own agendas is effectively creating the outlines of separate fiefs.

2013-05-17.title: Debating the Legal Basis for the War on Terror
2013-05-17.abstract: A Pentagon official urged Congress not to modify the statute providing the legal basis for battling the perpetrators of 9/11.

2013-05-18.title: Rights Group Says It Finds Proof of Torture in Syria
2013-05-18.abstract: Human Rights Watch said Friday that visits to two Syrian security centers contained proof of widespread, arbitrary detentions and torture by the government of President Bashar al-Assad.

2013-05-18.title: U.S.-Russian Diplomacy, With a Personal Touch
2013-05-18.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry and Russia’s foreign minister, Sergey V. Lavrov, have found a common purpose in trying to negotiate a Syria settlement, even though positions have not changed.

2013-05-18.title: The Salacious and the Sober Collide, Illustrating Hurdles for Italy’s Government
2013-05-18.abstract: On the same day, a young Moroccan woman testified in a sex scandal involving former Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi, and Italy’s new prime minister announced the suspension of a reviled tax.

2013-05-18.title: Trying Unlikely Comeback, Ex-Iran President Strikes Chord With Public
2013-05-18.abstract: Though once widely reviled, Ali Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani and his reputation as an economic pragmatist and modernizer seem to be resonating with the Iranian public.

2013-05-18.title: Toronto Mayor Is Besieged by Questions of Crack Use
2013-05-18.abstract: A video being shopped by two men with drug connections apparently shows Mayor Rob Ford of Toronto smoking a crack pipe.

2013-05-19.title: A Prison Site’s Future Stirs Up the Ghosts of Its Notorious Past
2013-05-19.abstract: With a peace center planned for Maze prison, home of an I.R.A. hunger strike, some in Northern Ireland are revisiting the complex story of the Troubles.

2013-05-19.title: On Hong Kong Shelves, Illicit Dirt on China’s Elite
2013-05-19.abstract: Visitors from mainland China turn to Hong Kong bookstores for forbidden delights: shelves of scandal-packed exposés about their Communist Party masters.

2013-05-19.title: Banned in China, but Popular in Hong Kong
2013-05-19.abstract: These are some of the books, prohibited in China, that have been selling well in bookstores in Hong Kong, according to booksellers.

2013-05-19.title: A Sampling of Pakistan’s Troubles
2013-05-19.abstract: A train journey across Pakistan presents sweeping landscapes and troubling images of a country plagued by natural disasters; sectarian, nationalist and Islamist violence; poverty; and corruption.

2013-05-19.title: Pakistan, as Seen Through Its Railways
2013-05-19.abstract: At every major stop on the long line from Peshawar to the turbulent port city of Karachi lie reminders of why the country is a worry to its people, and to the wider world.

2013-05-20.title: Slow Response by Georgians to Mob Attack on Gay Rally
2013-05-20.abstract: An inquiry into the attack that included rock-throwing priests caught on television cameras has led to no arrests.

2013-05-20.title: Dagestan’s Shadow War, Fought by ‘Many Tsarnaevs’
2013-05-20.abstract: A visit to Dagestan last year by Tamerlan Tsarnaev, a suspect in the Boston bombings, has drawn attention to the guerrilla conflict in the North Caucasus.

2013-05-20.title: Russia Expels Former American Embassy Official
2013-05-20.abstract: Thomas Firestone, a former Justice Department official who had been working in Moscow as a lawyer, was declared “persona non grata” this month, possibly because he had reportedly rebuffed an effort to recruit him as a spy.

2013-05-20.title: Indonesian Strongman’s Legacy Remains a Matter of Debate
2013-05-20.abstract: Suharto, the army leader turned president, resigned 15 years ago on Tuesday, and his legacy stirs conflicting emotions among Indonesians.

2013-05-20.title: Hackers From China Resume Attacks on U.S. Targets
2013-05-20.abstract: A cyberunit of the People’s Liberation Army in China appears to have resumed its attacks using different techniques, hitting several of the same victims it has gone after in the past.

2013-05-21.title: Egypt Sends Show of Force to Sinai After Kidnappings
2013-05-21.abstract: Dozens of tanks and hundreds of soldiers entered the largely lawless area that borders Israel and the Gaza Strip after gunmen kidnapped seven security officers.

2013-05-21.title: Polling Group in Russia Says It May Close
2013-05-21.abstract: After the Levada Center, the country’s only independent polling agency, was told it must register as a “foreign agent,” its director said fighting the requirement would effectively shut the center.

2013-05-21.title: Obama Couples Praise for Burmese Leader With Warning Against Violence
2013-05-21.abstract: President Obama encouraged the political and economic reforms in Myanmar, but warned the country’s visiting leader, President Thein Sein, that violence against minority Muslims had to stop.

2013-05-21.title: Applying Early Lessons to Build Afghan Security
2013-05-21.abstract: As Brig. Gen. Don Bolduc, deputy commander of the Special Operations forces in Afghanistan, prepares to leave the country, a program created to train villagers to protect their homes from insurgents will be his legacy there.

2013-05-21.title: State Dept. Report Says Countries Have Repressed Religious Freedom With Laws
2013-05-21.abstract: In an annual report, the State Department singled out nations that abused laws on blasphemy and apostasy to harass political opponents.

2013-05-22.title: Miami Workers Bribed, and Shortchanged, Venezuelan Banker, U.S. Says
2013-05-22.abstract: An official of a government bank in Caracas and two Americans were charged in Manhattan in what officials called a $66 million bribery-kickback scheme.

2013-05-22.title: A Group Taking Politics and Military Strategy to the Same Extremes
2013-05-22.abstract: A suicide bombing in Kabul was almost as noteworthy for the group that took credit — Hezb-i-Islami, a largely spent insurgent force — as for its deadliness.

2013-05-22.title: Genocide Trial of Guatemalan Ex-Dictator May Have to Restart
2013-05-22.abstract: Lawyers on both sides said Gen. Efraín Ríos Montt’s entire trial would most likely have to be repeated before a new panel of judges after a court threw out his conviction.

2013-05-22.title: Iran and Hezbollah Support for Syria Complicates Peace-Talk Strategy
2013-05-22.abstract: The stepped-up support President Bashar al-Assad of Syria has received from Iran and Hezbollah appears to have fortified his belief that he can hang on to power and prevail militarily.

2013-05-22.title: As Chinese Leader’s Visit Nears, U.S. Is Urged to Allow Counterattacks on Hackers
2013-05-22.abstract: As President Obama prepares to meet with President Xi Jinping of China, a commission is recommending steps that could raise the cost of the theft of American industrial secrets.

2013-05-23.title: Iran Is Seen Advancing Nuclear Bid
2013-05-23.abstract: International nuclear inspectors said Iran had increased its capabilities, but their report suggested that Tehran had not passed a “red line” that could incite military action.

2013-05-23.title: Europe Pushes to Shed Stigma of a Tax Haven
2013-05-23.abstract: From Luxembourg to the British Virgin Islands, the authorities are scrambling to  figure out how to change their secretive ways in banking without driving away lucrative foreign clients.

2013-05-23.title: One Drone Victim’s Trail From Raleigh to Pakistan
2013-05-23.abstract: Jude Kenan Mohammad was killed in what the C.I.A. calls a  “signature strike,” an attack based on patterns of activity, in Pakistan in 2011.

2013-05-23.title: U.S. Fears the Fighting in Syria Might Spill Over Into Lebanon, Kerry Says
2013-05-23.abstract: The decision by Hezbollah to fight for the Syrian government, and the prospect that Syrian rebels might fight Hezbollah operatives in Lebanon, has alarmed the Obama administration.

2013-05-23.title: Support for Kerry’s Mideast Peace Efforts on Eve of Visit
2013-05-23.abstract: Since becoming secretary of state, John Kerry has worked to jump-start the stalled peace process, embracing a seemingly intractable issue.

2013-05-24.title: Bid in France to Add Courses in English Raises Fear for Language
2013-05-24.abstract: An effort to draw more foreign students to French universities has led to worries about “Americanization disguised as globalization.”

2013-05-24.title: Accolades for German Party on Anniversary, but Votes? Perhaps Not
2013-05-24.abstract: The Social Democratic Party of Germany has a storied past, but a cloudy future, with polls showing it trailing behind the center-right Christian Democratic Union.

2013-05-24.title: Obama Lifts Moratorium on Transfer of Detainees
2013-05-24.abstract: It was not clear whether President Obama’s aim of shuttering the prison in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, would be achieved soon, but he began a renewed push to winnow down its population.

2013-05-24.title: Suicide Bombings in Niger Kill Dozens in Dual Strikes
2013-05-24.abstract: The attacks on a military base and a uranium mine were the first in Niger, which has taken a leading role against jihadism in West Africa.

2013-05-24.title: New Strife in Darfur Leaves Many Seeking Refuge
2013-05-24.abstract: A recent surge in fighting in Darfur has led to an unnerving increase in civilian upheaval, displacing nearly 300,000 people, according to the United Nations.

2013-05-25.title: Messy Fight Over Mandela Trust Goes Public
2013-05-25.abstract: Two of Nelson Mandela’s daughters are suing for more control of their father’s trust, bringing into the open a long-simmering dispute over his financial legacy.

2013-05-25.title: For Obama’s Global Vision, Daunting Problems
2013-05-25.abstract: Taking America off “perpetual war footing,” as the president proposes to do, will not be a simple task.

2013-05-25.title: Battle for Syrian Town Spurs Sectarian Fighting in Northern Lebanon
2013-05-25.abstract: Fighting for the Syrian town of Qusayr has been unusually fierce, and the tenacity of rebels against better-armed opponents has given the battle strong symbolic significance.

2013-05-25.title: Russia: Earthquake Hits Eastern Coast
2013-05-25.abstract: An 8.3-magnitude earthquake struck Russia’s Far East on Friday, sending tremors that prompted evacuations as far away as Moscow, more than 4,000 miles to the west of the epicenter.

2013-05-25.title: France: Investigators Tie I.M.F. Chief to Fraud Inquiry Involving Sarkozy
2013-05-25.abstract: Christine Lagarde, the managing director of the International Monetary Fund, was made an “assisted witness” on Friday in an investigation into allegations of possible fraud and misuse of public funds.

2013-05-26.title: Yemen Making Strides in Transition to Democracy After Arab Spring
2013-05-26.abstract: For more than two months, a cross section of Yemen’s population has gathered, trying to hammer out the shape a future government will take.

2013-05-26.title: U.S. Shift Poses Risk to Pakistan
2013-05-26.abstract: An American withdrawal from Afghanistan and the curtailment of the drone campaign could plunge Pakistan’s hotbed tribal areas into further chaos.

2013-05-26.title: Japanese Lab Workers Exposed to Low Levels of Radiation
2013-05-26.abstract: While the leak was small, it received intensive coverage in Japan’s news media, a reflection of the country’s anxiety over nuclear safety.

2013-05-26.title: A Supporting Role
2013-05-26.abstract: In a recent mission, the Americans adjusted to a new role, biting their tongues even as they followed Afghans into what they saw as dubious missions.

2013-05-26.title: In Afghan Transition, U.S. Forces Take a Step Back
2013-05-26.abstract: As the United States military moves into a support role in Afghanistan, a week spent with a brigade accompanying Afghan forces offered a direct look at the evolving training mission, for better or for worse.

2013-05-27.title: For New Breed of Rustlers, Nothing Is Sacred
2013-05-27.abstract: With meat eating on the rise in India, cows — considered sacred in Hinduism — are being stolen off the street by cattle rustlers and sold to illegal slaughterhouses.

2013-05-27.title: Guards in India Were Outgunned, Attack Survivor Says
2013-05-27.abstract: The attack in Chhattisgarh State, in which 24 people were killed, was carried out by Maoist insurgents.

2013-05-27.title: Who Owns This Land? In Greece, Who Knows?
2013-05-27.abstract: With no national land registry in the country, competing claims abound and taxes remain unpaid in a system based on handwritten ledgers.

2013-05-27.title: British Officials Knew Suspect in Soldier’s Death Had Ties to Al Qaeda
2013-05-27.abstract: Authorities said they knew that Michael Adebolajo, a suspect in the murder of an off-duty soldier last week, had been arrested in Kenya on suspicion of planning to join an extremist group.

2013-05-27.title: Deal Reached to Reduce Inequality in Colombia
2013-05-27.abstract: In an encouraging step in the country’s long-running guerrilla war, the government and a top rebel group reached a deal on rural inequality, an area of focus for the rebels.

2013-05-28.title: By Inserting Itself Into Syrian War, Hezbollah Makes Dramatic Gamble
2013-05-28.abstract: Hezbollah is betting its prestige and security by taking its followers on a new mission to crush the Syrian rebellion, an effort that could bring more power or defeat.

2013-05-28.title: Brazil: Mayor Punches Resident in Face
2013-05-28.abstract: The mayor of Rio de Janeiro, Eduardo Paes, punched a constituent in the face after being called the Portuguese equivalent of “excrement” in a dispute at a restaurant.

2013-05-28.title: In Terror Shift, Obama Took a Long Path
2013-05-28.abstract: President Obama’s decision to rewrite the playbook on counterterrorism policy dates to his re-election concerns, and it represents an effort to shape his legacy and to set the rules for his successors.

2013-05-28.title: Japan: U.S. Pilot Rescued Off Okinawa
2013-05-28.abstract: Japanese rescuers saved an American fighter pilot from the waters off southern Japan after his F-15 fighter crashed into the ocean early Tuesday, Japanese officials said.

2013-05-28.title: IHT Quick Read: May 28
2013-05-28.abstract: Europe ends Syrian arms embargo; an electricity crisis in Pakistan; Club Med gets a buyout offer; internships in China draw more foreigners; and trouble for cricket’s most lucrative competition.

2013-05-29.title: With Few Clues, Afghan Pathologists Try to Put Names to Torture Victims
2013-05-29.abstract: Using outdated equipment and sometimes faced with mangled remains, doctors seek to identify Afghans who were detained by a Special Forces team and disappeared.

2013-05-29.title: Anti-West Hard-Liner Gains in Iranian Race
2013-05-29.abstract: Saeed Jalili, a protégé of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, is the establishment’s favorite in the presidential election, an unsettling prospect for relations with the West.

2013-05-29.title: A Front-Runner Emerges in Iran’s Election
2013-05-29.abstract: Saeed Jalili, 47, who many say has been groomed for a top position in Iran’s establishment, is by far the most outspoken hardliner among the eight candidates approved to run.

2013-05-29.title: In Thailand’s Schools, Vestiges of Military Rule
2013-05-29.abstract: Some students are rebelling against a system they compare to “a factory that manufactures identical people,” and they have an ally in the education minister.

2013-05-29.title: 2 American Embassy Officials Are Shot in Venezuela Strip Club
2013-05-29.abstract: A brawl at the club in the basement of a Caracas shopping center left the two officials with injuries not considered life-threatening.

2013-05-30.title: Prosecutor in Argentina Sees Iranian Plot in Latin America
2013-05-30.abstract: Alberto Nisman, who is investigating the 1994 bombing of a Jewish center, said Iran had set up intelligence stations in Latin America to carry out terrorist attacks directly or through Hezbollah.

2013-05-30.title: Rodman Not First Choice for North Korea Trip
2013-05-30.abstract: The creators of “Vice,” an HBO newsmagazine, said the former basketball star Dennis Rodman was asked to visit North Korea after Michael Jordan said no.

2013-05-30.title: Salvadoran Court Denies Abortion to Ailing Woman
2013-05-30.abstract: In a 4-to-1 ruling, El Salvador’s highest court said that “the rights of the mother cannot be privileged over those” of the fetus. But the woman’s lawyer described the decision as “misogynistic.”

2013-05-30.title: Chances of U.N. Peace Talks on Syria Appear Dim as Both Sides Dig In
2013-05-30.abstract: As Syria’s foreign minister said President Bashar al-Assad would remain in office until elections next year and might even seek another term, the main opposition coalition said Mr. Assad must “step down.”

2013-05-30.title: Venezuela: New Details in Strip Club Shooting
2013-05-30.abstract: The State Department said that one of the men wounded was a member of the United States armed services and the other was a civilian.

2013-05-31.title: Comeuppance for the Rich, at Least on the Screen
2013-05-31.abstract: “We Are the Nobles,” the director Gaz Alazraki’s satirical look at the habits of wealthy Mexicans, has become the highest-grossing Mexican film ever.

2013-05-31.title: El Salvador: Doctors Can Induce Birth to Save Woman, Official Says
2013-05-31.abstract: A 22-year-old woman who was denied an abortion by El Salvador’s Supreme Court can terminate her pregnancy at the first sign of danger, the health minister said Thursday.

2013-05-31.title: Grim Task of Identifying Factories’ Dead Overwhelms Bangladeshi Lab
2013-05-31.abstract: Anger over the Rana Plaza collapse has intensified the pressure on a small lab struggling to make DNA matches with the remains of about 300 unidentified victims.

2013-05-31.title: U.S. Combat Commanders Should Handle War Zone Investigations, Panel Says
2013-05-31.abstract: After flaws in the case of Marines accused in a 2005 Iraq massacre prompted a review, a Defense Department panel proposed that American troops should not be investigated by the branches in which they serve.

2013-05-31.title: Vatican Bank Looks to Shed Its Image as an Offshore Haven
2013-05-31.abstract: The bank, long shrouded in mystery, has been under pressure to bring its practices in line with European norms to curb money laundering and terrorist financing.
